page: Amazon Web Services m8in.large is a general-purpose, network- and EBS-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 1 dedicated physical core, utilizing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance does not include local NVMe or SSD storage, meaning all storage operations rely on network-attached Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring users to architect systems around network-attached storage latency and throughput limits. However, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without the performance variability associated with shared-core or burstable instances.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8in.large offers a balanced entry-point configuration within the Gen8 Intel-based family, providing dedicated compute resources and optimized network capabilities for small-scale workloads. It is designed for general-purpose workloads such as lightweight web serving, small databases, containerized microservices, and basic development or testing environments that require stable CPU performance and moderate memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services m8in.large is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel processor and built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 dedicated physical core, 8.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage, relying entirely on network-attached EBS volumes for storage operations. This hardware profile provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, ensuring consistent compute performance through dedicated CPU allocation. The m8in.large is designed for workloads such as lightweight web serving, small database instances, containerized microservices, and development environments that require stable processing capabilities without the overhead of larger instance classes.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8in.large is a general-purpose x86_64 instance featuring a 3.9 GHz Intel processor, 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8in.large is a general-purpose x86_64 instance with a 3.9 GHz Intel CPU, 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU Intel instance offering balanced memory and optimized network performance for general-purpose workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz
2 vCPUs and 1 dedicated physical core with 8.0 GB RAM
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage volumes
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor for dedicated resource allocation
Suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and small databases
